synopsis: TE Stuart Merriam, a minister transferred from PCUSA to Tennessee Valley Presbytery in 1986, was subject to ecclesiastical discipline based on his criminal conviction in Papua New Guinea. The SJC sustained Merriam's appeal, finding that TVP violated due process principles and procedures under the BCO, particularly by failing to appoint counsel and conducting an inadequate trial. The case was remanded to TVP for a new trial following proper BCO procedures.
